narrative_blocks.header: Carried William Russell sea chest into Larned marriage
narrative_blocks.body: Abigail Russell took the William Russell Sea Chest with her on marrying Thaddeus Larned in the 1780s — the family heirloom passing into the Larned line through her marriage. Later genealogical accounts misattributed the transfer to 'Esther Russell,' but documentation through her son George Larned (born 1792) establishes that Abigail, not Esther, was the bride who carried the chest forward. The chest remains a tracked Hooker-line artifact today.

narrative_blocks.header: Died age thirty at Thompson, Connecticut, 1797
narrative_blocks.body: Abigail Russell Larned died March 7, 1797 in Thompson Connecticut at age thirty — six years after her son George Larned's 1792 birth and ten years into her marriage. The short life at age thirty was typical of late-eighteenth-century childbearing-age mortality. She is buried at West Thompson Cemetery in Thompson Connecticut.

narrative_blocks.header: Thompson CT 1766 to 1797; one documented son
narrative_blocks.body: Abigail Russell was born July 20, 1766 in Thompson Connecticut and died March 7, 1797 there at age thirty. Her one documented child was George Larned (born 1792). She married Thaddeus Larned (born 1756, died 1818) — Thaddeus outlived her by twenty-one years.

sources.short: Online vital records / New Haven Daily Morning Journal 1888

cross_connections.display_label: George Larned married his first cousin Harriet Russell in 1835.

George Larned married his first cousin Harriet Russell in 1835.

narrative_blocks.header: Son closed a 250-year sea chest circle
narrative_blocks.body: Their son George Huntington Larned donated the 1638 William Russell Sea Chest to the New Haven Colony Historical Society on 19 April 1888 — the 250th anniversary of its arrival in New Haven. The donation closed a quarter-millennium of continuous Hooker family custody in a single ceremonial act.

narrative_blocks.header: Married first cousin Harriet Russell at forty-three
narrative_blocks.body: In 1835 — thirteen years after his first wife Maria Read died — he married his first cousin Harriet Russell of Middletown CT. Both were grandchildren of Rev Noadiah Russell: George through Abigail Russell Larned, Harriet through Matthew Talcott Russell. The cousin marriage united two branches of the same Russell family.

narrative_blocks.header: Cotton-mill owner who retired to Wickford
narrative_blocks.body: He operated Larned's mill in Thompson CT through the 1820s-1850s and retired to North Kingstown (Wickford) Rhode Island in his later years. He died at Wickford in October 1874 at eighty-two — buried back at West Thompson Cemetery beside his father's people.
